mnemonics.title: Midpoint Decision Rule
mnemonics.content:
P<0: Choose E (East), P = P + 2x + 1
P≥0: Choose SE (Southeast), P = P + 2x - 2y + 1
Remember: 'Negative → East, Non-negative → Southeast'

mnemonics.title: Initial P Value
mnemonics.content:
P₀ = 1 - r for radius r
Think: 'One minus Radius gets you started'

mnemonics.title: Eight-Way Symmetry Points
mnemonics.content:
From (x,y), derive: (y,x), (y,-x), (x,-y), (-x,-y), (-y,-x), (-y,x), (-x,y)
Pattern: Swap and negate in systematic pairs

acronyms.usage: Used in midpoint circle algorithm to select between candidate pixels. P = x² + y² - r²
The pixel to the right of current position (x+1, y)
Efficient integer arithmetic circle drawing algorithm

visualTips.visualization: Imagine dividing circle into 8 pizza slices. Draw one slice, then mirror it to get the rest.
Picture a line between two candidate pixels. If midpoint is inside circle boundary, pick the inner pixel.
E = move horizontally right, SE = move diagonally down-right. Choose based on which keeps you closer to true circle.

analogies.analogy: Like walking around a round pond choosing the best stepping stones - always pick the stone that keeps you closest to the water's edge.
Like having 8 mirrors around one point - see one reflection and you know what all 8 look like.
Like a fitness score - if it's negative (good), stay the course; if positive (bad), adjust direction.
